mnemonics.title: Exit Status Codes
mnemonics.content: ZERO SUCCESS - 0 means success. Non-zero means failure (NZ-F): 1=General Error, 2=Misuse, 126=Not Executable, 127=Not Found, 128+N=Signal N. Remember: '0 for OK, Check the Docs for the Rest'.

mnemonics.title: Remember $?
mnemonics.content: $? is like a 'receipt' - it shows the 'status' of the last 'question' (command) you asked. Check it immediately or the next command overwrites it!

mnemonics.title: EXIT Trap
mnemonics.content: EXIT = Execute When Terminating - E-W-T. Think 'Everything Will Terminate' - runs cleanup code.

acronyms.usage: Holds exit status of most recent foreground command. Check immediately after command execution.
Special trap that runs when script exits, used for cleanup.
Makes pipeline return rightmost failed command's exit status instead of just last command.

visualTips.visualization: Think of a thermometer: 0 is 'cool/success' at bottom, 1-255 are 'heat levels' of different failures. 127 is 'hot/not found'.
Signal exit code = 128 + signal number. Visualize: 128 is a 'base camp', add the signal number to reach the 'peak'.
$? is like a sticky note - write down the result, but it gets overwritten by the next person (command) who walks by.
Default: only last command matters. With pipefail: the 'weakest link' (first failure) determines the status.

analogies.analogy: Like a doctor's 'Fit' certificate - 0 means 'All Clear, No Problems'.
Like different error codes on a car's dashboard - each number tells you what went wrong.
Like a fire alarm's 'All Clear' at the end of a building evacuation - runs regardless of how you left.
Like a security camera that records only the last few seconds - you must check it right after the event.
Like a chain in a bicycle - the chain breaks at the weakest link, that's the status you get.
